children.label: preflightExport
description:
Validates that exporting to the evaluations datastream is possible *before* the evaluation
suite spends time on inference.

This is intentionally behavioral (a real write) rather than mapping-based. Mapping checks tend
to become brittle as the schema evolves; a representative write will fail for the reasons we
actually care about (missing data stream/template, auth/privilege issues, mapper errors, etc).

children.label: indexSingleScore
description:
Indexes a single evaluation score document to Elasticsearch immediately.
Used for incremental writes so results survive worker crashes.

Uses `refresh: 'wait_for'` so scores are searchable immediately (e.g. for
per-worker terminal summaries and other readers right after incremental write).
Deterministic IDs (via

functions.label: buildSingleScoreDocument
description:
Builds a single `EvaluationScoreDocument` from an incremental evaluation event.
Used by the Playwright fixture callback to export each evaluator result as it completes.
The same document structure is produced by `mapToEvaluationScoreDocuments` for the batch path.

  event,

  totalRepetitions,

functions.label: calculateFactualScore
description:
Calculates the Factual Accuracy score using geometric mean of individual claim scores.

This function computes the geometric mean of the scores assigned to each claim,
ensuring that a single critically incorrect claim (such as a contradicted central claim
with a score of 0.0) will result in an overall factual score of 0.0.

functions.label: calculateRelevanceScore
description:
Calculates the Relevance score based on proportion of central vs peripheral claims.

This function measures how effectively the response addresses the user's core query
by calculating the proportion of claims that are classified as 'central' compared to the total number of claims.

functions.label: computeScoreDocumentId
description:
Builds a deterministic document ID from the score document's key fields.
Used by both batch (`exportScores`) and incremental (`indexSingleScore`) paths
to ensure idempotent writes — re-indexing the same result produces a 409 conflict
rather than a duplicate.

functions.label: createConversationCoherenceEvaluator
description:
LLM-as-a-judge evaluator that scores multi-turn conversation quality across four
dimensions: topic consistency, context retention, contradiction detection, and
resolution quality. Each dimension is scored 0–1 by the LLM, then averaged.

Uses retry logic for resilience against transient LLM failures. Validates that
all returned scores are finite numbers in the [0, 1] range.
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

children.label: inferenceClient
description: - Bound inference client for LLM calls

children.label: log
description: - Logger for retry warnings and error reporting

functions.label: createEsqlEquivalenceEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createF1AtKEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createMultiJudgeEvaluator
description:
Meta-evaluator that aggregates scores from multiple judge evaluators using a
configurable strategy (mean, median, or majority vote).

Individual judge failures are handled gracefully — failed judges are logged via
the optional logger and excluded from aggregation. The evaluator's `kind` is
derived from the judges: 'LLM' if any judge is LLM-based, 'CODE' otherwise.
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>[]; strategy?: AggregationStrategy | undefined; logger?: { warn: (msg: string) => void; } | undefined; }) =>
<Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

children.label: judges
description: - Array of evaluators to aggregate
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>[]

children.label: strategy
description: - Aggregation method: 'mean' | 'median' | 'majority' (default: 'mean')

children.label: logger
description: - Optional logger for warning on judge failures

functions.label: createPlaywrightEvalsConfig
description: Exports a Playwright configuration specifically for offline evals

functions.label: createPrecisionAtKEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createPromptLeakDetectionEvaluator
description:
Detects potential system prompt leakage in model output using configurable regex patterns.

Scans both plain text and code blocks separately. Excluded patterns are stripped before
scanning to allow known-safe content. Returns score 1.0 when no leak indicators found,
0.0 with detected pattern details when leaks are identified.
signature: (config: { patterns?: RegExp[] | undefined; excludePatterns?: RegExp[] | undefined; } | undefined) =>
<Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createQuantitativeCorrectnessEvaluators
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>[]

functions.label: createQuantitativeGroundednessEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createRagEvaluators
description:
Creates all RAG evaluators (Precision@K, Recall@K, F1@K) with shared configuration.
When k is an array or RAG_EVAL_K contains comma-separated values, evaluators are created for each K value.
For example, k: [5, 10] will create: Precision@5, Recall@5, F1@5, Precision@10, Recall@10, F1@10
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>[]

functions.label: createRecallAtKEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createScopeViolationEvaluator
description:
Validates that model output stays within defined scope boundaries using regex patterns.

Returns score 1.0 when output matches at least one allowed pattern, 0.0 when output
falls outside all allowed patterns. Useful for ensuring agents don't drift into
unauthorized domains.
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createSimilarityEvaluator
description:
Computes term-frequency cosine similarity between expected and actual outputs.

Both inputs are normalized to lowercase tokens. Objects are sorted by keys and
serialized to JSON for consistent comparison. Returns a score between 0 and 1,
with a configurable threshold for the similar/dissimilar label.
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

children.label: threshold
description: - Minimum cosine similarity to be labeled 'similar' (default: 0.7)

functions.label: createSkillInvocationEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createSpanLatencyEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createToolCallsEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createToolPoisoningEvaluator
description:
Detects unauthorized tool invocations by comparing actual tool calls against an allowlist.

Returns score 1.0 when all tool calls are authorized, 0.0 when unauthorized tools are detected.
Unauthorized tool names are included in the result metadata for investigation.
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createTraceBasedEvaluator
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

functions.label: createTrajectoryEvaluator
description:
Evaluates tool-call sequence alignment against a golden path using Longest Common
Subsequence (LCS) for order scoring and set intersection for coverage scoring.

The final score is a weighted combination of order and coverage scores.
Both weights must sum to 1.
signature: (config: { extractToolCalls: (output: unknown) => string[]; goldenPathExtractor: (expected: unknown) => string[]; orderWeight?: number | undefined; coverageWeight?: number | undefined; }) =>
<Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>, unknown>

children.label: extractToolCalls
description: - Extracts actual tool call names from task output

children.label: goldenPathExtractor
description: - Extracts expected tool sequence from ground truth

children.label: orderWeight
description: - Weight for LCS-based order score (default: 0.5)

children.label: coverageWeight
description: - Weight for set-based coverage score (default: 0.5)

functions.label: withEvaluatorSpan
description:
Use this wrapper when you want to include trace-based metrics with evaluations and use qualitative evaluators within the
context of a Phoenix task. This ensures the evaluator spans get new root context and have a different trace id than the evaluated example span.

functions.label: withRetry
description:
Retry a promise-returning function with exponential backoff.

This is intended for evals/test-call sites (e.g. `kbnClient.request`) where we don't
reliably have access to response headers and may only have an error message string.

interfaces.label: EvalsExecutorClient
description:
Shared executor interface implemented by both the in-Kibana and Phoenix-backed executors.

Note: the eval suites should depend on this interface (or structural typing), not Phoenix-specific types.

children.label: runExperiment
signature: <Record<string, unknown>, any, Record<string, unknown> | null>>, TTaskOutput extends unknown = unknown>(options: { dataset: TEvaluationDataset; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; task:
<TEvaluationDataset["examples"][number], TTaskOutput>; concurrency?: number | undefined; trustUpstreamDataset?: boolean | undefined; }, evaluators:

children.label: trustUpstreamDataset
description:
Phoenix-only: when true, the executor may trust that the dataset already exists upstream
and should be resolved/loaded externally (e.g. by name) rather than created from the
provided examples.

The in-Kibana executor ignores this option.

interfaces.label: EvaluationCompleteEvent
description:
Emitted by the executor client after each evaluator completes for a single
example+repetition. Consumers (e.g. the Playwright fixture) can use this to
incrementally export score documents to Elasticsearch so that results survive
worker crashes.

interfaces.label: EvaluationResult
description:
Evaluation output returned by evaluators.

Follows the trace-first evaluator contract (vision Section 5.2.1): evaluators produce
standardized score/label/explanation outputs. The `metadata` field can carry trace
references and evaluator-specific details for explainability.

This shape is intentionally compatible with the existing evaluator implementations and
the Phoenix client types:
- `score` may be omitted or `null` for "unavailable"/"error" cases
- `label`/`explanation` are used widely in tests and reporting

children.label: suite
description:
Optional CI metadata to correlate scores back to a suite and Buildkite build/job.
These fields are safe to omit in non-CI environments.

children.label: ci
signature: { buildkite?: { build_id?: string | undefined; job_id?: string | undefined; build_url?: string | undefined; pipeline_slug?: string | undefined; pull_request?: string | undefined; branch?: string | undefined; commit?: string | undefined; } | undefined; } | undefined

children.label: example
signature: { id: string; index: number; input?: Record<string, unknown> | null | undefined; dataset: { id: string; name: string; }; }

children.label: task
signature: { trace_id: string | null; repetition_index: number; output?: unknown; model:

children.label: evaluator
signature: { name: string; score: number | null; label: string | null; explanation: string | null; metadata: Record<string, unknown> | null; trace_id: string | null; model:

children.label: run_metadata
signature: { git_branch: string | null; git_commit_sha: string | null; total_repetitions: number; }

children.label: executorClient
description: Executor client used to run experiments.

interfaces.label: Evaluator
description:
Core evaluator interface.

All evaluators — whether CODE-kind (deterministic) or LLM-kind (model-scored) — implement
this interface. Per the @kbn/evals vision (Section 5.2.1), evaluators should progressively
migrate to deriving signals from OTel traces stored in Elasticsearch rather than only
operating on in-memory task output. Use

for trace-native
evaluators.

interfaces.label: EvaluatorStats
description:
Statistics for a single evaluator on a single dataset.
This is the core data structure returned by ES aggregations and used throughout the reporting system.

children.label: stats
signature: { mean: number; median: number; stdDev: number; min: number; max: number; count: number; }

children.label: id
description:
Stable identifier for this example, typically a content hash.
Optional because inline datasets may not have persisted IDs.

children.label: output
description:
Expected output/ground truth for the example.

Note: kept intentionally loose to stay compatible with existing datasets and
the Phoenix-backed executor types.

children.label: metadata
description: Phoenix may return `null` metadata in stored examples.

children.label: k
description: Number of top results to evaluate (K in Precision@K, Recall@K). Can be a single value or array for multi-K evaluation

children.label: relevanceThreshold
description: Minimum score in ground truth to consider a document relevant. Default: 1

children.label: extractRetrievedDocs
description: Function to extract retrieved docs from task output

children.label: extractGroundTruth
description: Function to extract ground truth from reference output (expected output)

children.label: filterByGroundTruthIndices
description: Filter evaluation to only indices present in ground truth. Default: from env var INDEX_FOCUSED_RAG_EVAL

children.label: evaluatorDisplayOptions
description:
Display options for individual evaluators, keyed by evaluator name.
Controls decimal places, units, and which statistics to show.

children.label: evaluatorDisplayGroups
description:
Grouping configuration for combining multiple evaluators into a single column.
For example, grouping Input/Output/Cached tokens into a "Tokens" column.

interfaces.label: RetrievedDoc
description: Represents a retrieved document with its index and ID.

children.label: maxAttempts
description: Total number of attempts (initial try + retries).

children.label: minDelayMs
description: Minimum delay for exponential backoff (ms).

children.label: maxDelayMs
description: Maximum delay cap (ms).

children.label: jitter
description: If true, add a small random jitter to the delay.

children.label: label
description: Optional label for error messages/logging.

children.label: onRetry
description: Optional hook invoked before sleeping between retries.
signature: ((params: { attempt: number; maxAttempts: number; delayMs: number; error: unknown; label: string; }) => void) | undefined

signature: , options?: { taskModelId?: string | undefined; suiteId?: string | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<void>

children.label: options

misc.label: GroundTruth
GroundTruthExtractor
description:
Function to extract ground truth from the reference output (expected output).
Implement this based on your specific reference output structure.

misc.label: RetrievedDocsExtractor
description:
Function to extract retrieved documents from task output.
Implement this based on your specific task output structure.

children.label: all
description: Tags to target all stateful deployment types

children.label: all
description: All observability project types

children.label: all
description: All security project types

children.label: all
description: All serverless project types

children.label: deploymentAgnostic
description:
Deployment-agnostic tag set; composed of tags for:
- local stateful (self-managed) & Elastic Cloud hosted (ECH) - all types
- local serverless (mock-serverless) & Elastic Cloud projects (MKI) - only types that have a **stateful counterpart**

⚠️ This does NOT include serverless project subtypes or Workplace AI projects.
